transcript: Hey, listen, guy, I live adjacent to you and there’s a buncha rope and belongings of mine that went missing. And I need to talk to you a little bit about what’s goin’ on up here. [exhales]
Well, there was a big thing o’ sawdust coverin’ up some rope. Sawdust was gone, the rope was gone!—
—[pitch-up] But I suppose you don’t know anything about that, huh?—
—[pitch-up] Sawdust was gone! The rope was gone! [exhales]
Sawdust was gone, rope was gone!
[synth saxophone] I had some puddin’ out there, too—that it was just made—and I don’t suppose you know anything about that, either, do ye?! [delay & pitch]—
I talked to my letter-carrier about ya. He says he saw someone—
—matching your description up ‘ere. Now whatchu tryin’ ta do up ‘ere?! [delay & pitch]
Ooh, boy! I—I know it’s not gonna—
—I’m lookin’ for answers, here, today. I’m lookin’ for answers and that’s all’s I need! I don’t need a lot of your fiddle-faddle, here!—
—Well I wanted to get the pudding—I'm at—I—well, I’m usin’ the rope. I’m gonna put it back. I’ll put the rope back someday. But not today.
Just like you found it, right?
I don’t know what you’re talking about—
—[to Andrew] Who are you talking to?
Um. This is Andrew. How’s—how’s your evening?
Um. I—uh—uh, do you—do you know how we got here?
—That's—uh! [chuckles] Um, I’m not sure if I did, actually. My phone just rang.
Oh. Well, so did mine. So somebody’s screwing around with our phones; that happens quite often—
—don’t let it bother you. We haven’t been able to trace it. And, ah, it’s just somebody having fun at our expense. So, have a good day!—
—I understand. I hope you’re doing well—
—and I have a great deal of love for you.
NBS Electronics. This is Fat Willie.
Yeah, I need to do a re-fret on a guitar. [exhales]
Uh, that, I won’t be able to help you with; I, uh, strictly concentrate on amplifiers.
Well, it’s got an amp in the guitar body, ya follow? It’s an old Sears Roebuck. [exhales]
Uh, actually, uh, the old Sears Roebuck guitar would have the amplifier in the case, not in the guitar.
No, it’s a red one. It’s got the little 10-inch speaker right on the body. It’s like an ES-335 shape. So it’s got a fat bottom-end of the body—
—O—okay, well, uh, be that as it may, I am not a luthier and I cannot do a fret job. I’m sorry, sir—
—Well I—I got crackle comin’ outta the speaker in the body of the guitar. And it does have a speaker in the guitar. Now surely you can check it out!—
—Look, jerk! Find some-fucking-body else to waste their time. you dickhead!
Yeah, could I place an order here, please? [exhales]
Certainly! What can I get for ya?
[pitch fx] Okay, now what kinda ribs d’you have up there available for pickup? Ah, for pickup? [delay]—
—For pickup, we got, uh, baby back ribs and we got spare ribs. Uh, they’re both—
Can I get about 96—uh, the spare ribs, please? [delay]

—Dude, 96? D’you understand it’s fuckin’ t—d’you understand that every restaurant is fuckin’ struggling to survive right now? I don’t need to fucking—you to waste my time 96 fuckin’ orders of spare ribs, okay? I know you’re tryin’ to have fun but fuck off! Okay?—
Seriously! Fuckin’ think about it! Okay? We’re fuckin’ swimmin’ here!—
—Okay, I’ll take down your credit card number right now, then. And we’ll have that right for ya. If you wanna give me that.
You use cash?! O! How convenient! Yeah! [inhales]
—Why don’t you come in and prepay and then we’ll cook the 96 orders of ribs for ya?
Well that’s not really convenient for me. I—I have some other items to order—
—That’s not “convenient” for you? I’m so sorry! You know what’s not convenient for me?
—Is you fuckin’ calling me on the fucking phone and wasting my fucking time when—
—I have, like, two shifts a week at a fuckin’ restaurant that pays me five dollars a fuckin’ hour? You know? In the middle of a global pandemic? You piece of shit!
I gotta eat, guy! Whadda ya think of that?
Yeah! You wanna eat 96 fuckin’ pounds o’ ribs?! Yeah—
Yeah! I’ll fuckin’ leg wrestle you! Shut the fuck up!
And we’ve got another caller. Looks like we’ve got Lang on line two. Good morning! How are you?
[tremolo] Good morning! Hey, did Wagner Wang have a tussle on the monkey bars or was that just a buncha conjecture from the Beltway? [exhales]
That’s—yeah, we haven’t talked about that much here. Uh, today’s topic, again, is “School”—uh, let’s keep that on track. We’ve mainly been pertaining to our local district.
[tremolo] Oh, okay! Well did ya hear that Rudy Guiliani tapped Bill Cosby to head up the Campaign for Voter Integrity?

Yeah, that doesn’t sound like it’ll be all that fruitful. Um. But y’know, while we’re on the topic of schools, I—I didn’t get your town on the note, here. Are you—are you from here in Apple City? Did you go to Apple City High?
[tremolo] I’m from Jiggs, Nevada. Born ’n reared! And I thought that tappin’ Bill Cosby was a great idea! [exhales] Phylicia Rashad is a coach here, in the whole thing—
—That’s the last Summit issue, I think. Yeah—
—O! [delay] Oh, big time! And then they got Lark Voorhies tapped! [exhales] They got her on tap, so it’s gonna be a heck of a Committee! [delay] Things’re gonna really—
—change in the Beltway, if you ask my opinion. So—
—Yeah, right? That’s what we’re here for: to hear your opinion—
—I just wanted to let ya know what I thought of it. I’m just a layperson, ya know. [tremolo] I’m wonderin’ what [delay] the real pundits thought o’ the whole situation up ‘ere!
Yeah! Absolutely! Well, thank you so much for the call and—and please, have a great morning out there, wherever ya are, huh?
Yes, Mr. Mario? This is Shaquille Frick with ASCAP Performing Rights Organization. How are we today, sir? [delay & pitch]
Oh, I’m closed. I don’t have any—
—I’m only open to take out. I haven’t had music since March 16th of 2020.
I’m actually calling about the 3rd and 4th quarter of 2019. Ah, we’ve got a small balance of 197 that we’ve just been tryin’ to collect.
I was paid up to date in my books, so…
Not according to my records. We’ve gotten three letters back that we sent to ya. Okay?—
—called ya three times and told ya I’ve been closed to that—that—that—that. And I was paid up to date! So, you know?
Hey, Mario, it’s not just all Jell-O shots and karaoke machines, okay? There’s a process in place for how this works. You follow?—
—and explained this to you. I’m paid up to date with my bank records—
Two o’ you guys I pay every six months—
—and one of youse I pay annually. And I did not have music, you know?
—So I’m not paying. When I re-open, I’ll let youse know and then we can start the licensing again.
Oh, we’re not gonna re-open at all unless we get this back balance taken care of! You’re in arrears, sir! I don’t know how many ways I’m supposed to put that!—
—Which company are you with? I wanna know. Because I’m gonna check my back records and then I’m gonna call you back.
Oh, you are? [delay] I’m at BMI and I work at ASCAP. I’m a Vice President.
I’ve been on the job about 18 days but, yes—
—Well I’m gonna check my r—bank records and then you can call me back tomorrow; how’s that?—
—Hey! Mario, I’m gonna go online, check ya out!—
—See what kinda place you’re runnin’ here! ‘Cause this isn’t how we operate. We’ve been in business for 144 years!—
Yes, this is LAMCO: Latin American Music Corporation. Okay?
That’s good that you called because I checked all my records and actually, you owe me money! Because I’m paid up on all o’ youse. So, tell you what, LANKO, go fuck yourself!
This is LAMCO. You played a song called “My Tostado” from Reuben Herrero 14 times in November of 2019. You have a balance of $344 with us! End o’ sentence!
Listen, asshole, I know you’re a bill collector and if you call me one more time, I’m gonna find out where ya are and I’m gonna pull your rectum up to your fuckin’ ears!
M—Mario, can you be professional, plea—? [delay]
Hi, yes! This is Mr. Frick with SESAC: Society of European Stage Authors and Composers. How are ya tonight, sir?—

—Listen, Mr. Prick. So far, you’ve been from fuckin’ CSAC, BMI, and ASCAP! I pulled my bank records! You—all three of youse actually owe me money. So, fuck you! Fuck Mr. Shaquille and fuck the other douchebag that called!
—[sfx] Hi! This is Ramon from Sound Exchange phoning.

And, uh, I know you’ve had some problems with our representative. So I just wanted to make sure we’re in good shape here tonight. [delay & pitch-up] And just give ya a quick call. We wanted to give you a coupon for $20 off, okay? Just so that we can bring your balance down.
Buddy, I know you’re a collection agency. I don’t owe them anything, “Ramon” or “Mr. Frick”—
—or whatever. I am not paying them!
No, sir. We wanna give ya a voucher so that you can play the song “O! Tostado!” by Ruben Herrero—[pitch-up] No charge! Royalty-free!—25 times—
—“Oh Tastado”—b—hey, why don’t you take Oh Tastado and toast it up your asshole?!
Hey, Jeff, what’s goin’ on, man? This is Tate. How ya doin’?
—I don’t—fuck off, man! I’m in bed!—
—I don’t know a goddamn “Tate”! Lose this number, asshole!
What the fuck do you want?!
—you wanna walk down here and get a cap in your goddamn ass?!
I’m your new neighbor. I just need your help—
—Yeah, fuck off! I don’t give a fuck! Why the fuck are you callin’ me?!
‘Cause I need your help, big guy [delay]—
—Fuck you! You want some help, bring your ass down here and I’ll fuckin’ put you in the goddamn ground!
—Quit fuckin! calling me, dickhead! Fuck you!—
—I’m gonna grab ‘hold o’ your jaw and push ya around. Whadda ya think of that? [exhales]
This is Chris calling from Electric Works. To whom am I speaking?
You’re speaking to Alex. Who is this? Chris?
Um, I was called by you guys. I’m Electric Works.
No. Somebody’s screwing with the phones. They—there’s—there’s a prankster out there who does that, who puts calls into people and has has them call back all over the country. Uh, just ignore it—
Yeah. Sorry about that. It’s happened to me many times.
Oh, my gosh! It must be so much fun for you!
Yeah, well!—well—so, just ignore it and just hang up whenever it—usually, he connects three people at the same time. I dont know how you—
—Oh! Well don’t you wanna wait and find out who else he’s gonna connect?
Oh, come on, Alex! Stay on the phone!
